article_title: Crystal 1.20 Ships an Experimental io_uring Event Loop, @[TargetFeature] Annotation, and an Overhauled Process API

content_preview.title: Crystal 1.20 Ships an Experimental io_uring Event Loop, @[TargetFeature] Annotation, and an Overhauled Process API
content_preview.summary: Crystal 1.20.0 lands with a Linux io_uring event loop, CPU-feature targeting via @[TargetFeature], a new array-based Process API, kernel TLS, and a fix for an HTTP request-smuggling vulnerability.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Crystal team has released version 1.20.0 of the Ruby-inspired, statically typed, ahead-of-time compiled language, the project announced on its [official blog](https://crystal-lang.org/2026/04/16/1.20.0-released/). The release is dated April 16, 2026 in the announcement post, and the corresponding tag on the [project's GitHub release page](https://github.com/crystal-lang/crystal/releases/tag/1.20.0) lists 23 contributors and a long list of compiler, runtime, and standard-library ...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong technical write-up (826 words). Conservative framing — explicitly highlights the team's own 'highly experimental' caveat on the io_uring loop and the 1.21 enablement timeline for execution contexts.

source_verification.https://crystal-lang.org/2026/04/16/1.20: source-0.html verified for: 1.20.0 release April 16, 2026, io_uring event loop with -Devloop=io_uring, 'highly experimental' caveat, SQPOLL with io_uring_sq_thread_idle=200, fiber-yielding scheduling change, @[TargetFeature] annotation, Process.capture and array-based Process API with example ['crystal', 'tool', 'format'], kernel TLS default on Linux/FreeBSD, mold/lld linker preference, M:N scheduling work toward 1.21. The blog says 161 changes by 21 contributors (article correctly attributes the '23 contributors' figure to GitHub release page, not the blog).
source_verification.https://github.com/crystal-lang/crystal/: source-1.html verified for: 23 contributors total ('Sija, ysbaddaden, and 21 other contributors' = 23), LLVM 22.1 and 23.0 support, @[TargetFeature], Process.capture/Process.run?/array-based Process.new, Crystal::Lock and Fiber::ExecutionContext::ThreadPool primitives, the 'HTTP::Server is no longer vulnerable to request smuggling' fix with GHSA-wqh5-7w63-pm68 advisory, Sec-WebSocket-Protocol validation tightening.
source_verification.https://forum.crystal-lang.org/t/crystal: source-2.html — corroborates the blog post and links readers to the canonical announcement and GitHub tag. Used appropriately as a third corroborating source for the security fix highlight.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All technical claims (release date, contributor count, io_uring flags, SQPOLL config, @[TargetFeature], Process API, kernel TLS, mold/lld, LLVM 22.1/23.0, M:N scheduling, request-smuggling advisory, Sec-WebSocket-Protocol validation) verified verbatim against snapshots.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and technical. The 'feature release rather than a marketing one' framing is editorial but accurate. 'What We Don't Know' section honestly flags the open questions (io_uring real-world performance, ecosystem adoption of @[TargetFeature], 1.21 production-readiness for execution contexts).
editor_notes.originality: Crystal 1.20.0 release not previously covered. No prior Crystal-language articles in the archive — first Crystal coverage on the site.
recommendations: Sources are official-project-and-GitHub primary sources; allowlist warning is informational only — these are appropriate primary sources for a software release story.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. High-quality technical News piece with primary-source verification, conservative framing, and full traceability between every claim and the cited snapshot.
